mnemonics: REFlexive, Symmetric, and Transitive: An equivalence relation must be REFerred to as a proper STamp of approval.
Partition Party: A partition is a party where the set is split into exclusive groups (equivalence classes) where every element is invited to exactly one group.
keyPoints: An equivalence relation on a set A is a relation that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
An equivalence relation partitions a set into disjoint subsets called equivalence classes.
Every partition of a set defines a unique equivalence relation, and vice versa.
commonMistakes: Assuming all relations that are symmetric and transitive are also reflexive (a relation can be symmetric and transitive on a subset of elements without being reflexive on the entire set).
Confusing the properties: Forgetting that 'symmetric' means if a~b then b~a, and 'transitive' means if a~b and b~c then a~c.
